analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0–2: Black–Scholes implied vol vs m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T) with T from round3description DTE mapping (CSV day 0→8d at open, 1→7, 2→6) plus intraday winding per plot_iv_smile_round3 (dte_effective). Vega per strike from BS vega(S,K,T,iv). Top-of-book spread width on extract and each VEV from bid_price_1/ask_price_1.
used_to_decide: Median extract spread ~5; deep OTM vouchers (e.g. VEV_4000) median spread ~21 vs ATM ~3–4 — motivates wider minimum quote width and optional skip/wing filter when edge is small. Vega-weighted ATM IV ~0.241 vs global median ~0.245; right-wing IV median higher than left — keep quadratic smile fit, update slowly with EWMA so fair does not chase tick noise.

analysis_description: Offline parameter sweep: EWMA alpha on vega-weighted polyfit coeff path (day 0, timestamp step 200). Mean L2 step size of (c2,c1,c0) vector vs alpha.
used_to_decide: Chose alpha=0.045 as balance between responsiveness and smoothness (between 0.04 and 0.06 in grid); paired with removing extract-only MM after v0 showed large extract losses.

analysis_description: Cross-check: v2 wing filter thresholds tied to iteration-0 spread medians by strike bucket; micro re-backtest vs v1.
used_to_decide: Small PnL gain vs v1 (+23 total); kept v2 as slightly better and simpler than a full new branch — documents that wing filter mainly helps day 1 VEV_4000.

analysis_description: IV-fair edge threshold study across days 0-2: per timestamp fit vega-weighted quadratic IV smile, compute abs(mid-fair) and half-spread; evaluate candidate take thresholds.
used_to_decide: At abs-edge>=2.0, ~98% of samples are in half-spread<=2 books, suggesting a plausible selective-taker opportunity. Implemented in v3 but backtest falsified the improvement hypothesis (large losses), so taker escalation is rejected for now.

analysis_description: Post-mortem of v3 losses by strike/day from backtest output.
used_to_decide: Losses concentrated in near-ATM strikes (especially VEV_5100/5000) on days 1-2 indicate overreaction to transient edge. Rolled back to conservative/no-taker variants.

analysis_description: Small parameter sweep around wing guard (EDGE_MULT_WIDE 0.85 -> 1.0) while preserving sticky-smile core.
used_to_decide: No aggregate gain vs v2 (exact tie). Tie-break by simplicity keeps v2 as preferred parent for next iteration.

analysis_description: VEV smile curvature stability vs extract EWMA rate: for each alpha, subsample (step 100) per day 0-2, fit vega-weighted quad IV in m_t with S = EWMA of extract mid, record std of poly coeff c2 (curvature) over time. Same BS IV/vega machinery as live trader.
used_to_decide: Grid selected alpha=0.045 to minimize c2 jiggle; implemented as trader_v6. Backtest: total PnL 19246 vs 19255 for v2, so the smoother anchor did not improve realized PnL here; next iterations should stay on tick-S smile (v2) unless a different smoothing hook is found.

analysis_description: Grid over smile-coeff EMA rate alpha in {0.02,0.03,0.045,0.06} on days 0-2, step 25: for each path, sequential EMA of poly coeffs from per-tick vega-weighted fit; at VEV_5200 record median |Delta fair| between steps and |BS call theta| (dV/dT, r=0) at sticky IV. Fair uses S from tape, T from dte_effective/365 (round3 DTE + intraday wind).
used_to_decide: Median |Delta fair| for VEV_5200 is ~2.0 and nearly flat in alpha in this grid; |theta| is huge (~1785/year) as expected for short T—dominant signal is moneyness/IV, not a small EMA tweak. Tried trader_v7 with EMA 0.03: backtest tied v2 at 19255; default remains v2 (0.045) for fewer moving parts unless future work needs slower reaction.

analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0-2, step 200: implied IV from mid per strike, then Black-Scholes call gamma = phi(d1)/(S*sigma*sqrt(T)). Median gamma peaks near VEV_5200/5300 (~0.0022); wings ~1e-5. Same DTE mapping as live trader.
used_to_decide: Added gamma-based quote widening in trader_v8 (scale 0.15 vs GAMMA_REF from ATM medians). Backtest PnL tied v2 at 19255: sub-tick adjustment often does not change integer prices after rounding, so prefer simpler v2 unless we scale gamma bump in whole ticks (e.g. 1-2) in a follow-up.
